digest.greeting: Wednesday, June 24, Jean. Today is anchored by the Jorge transition-plan conversation, the Yuri and Andrea 1:1s, LeadUp Module 2, the CoE Biweekly Touchpoint, and the Mar/Jhara quick touchpoint. Outlook shows 7 meetings plus 2 protected focus blocks, 2 carried-over items, 0 active reminders, and 1 urgent item. Keep the day centered on finishing the KPI SMART goals, updating Jorge's project sheet before Jill's June 30 portfolio meeting, deciding whether Mar gets PE support for MHE/RFE before the team transition, and keeping the TRACE follow-on in the right deferred/prioritization path. Broad Teams recent-thread discovery returned a Microsoft 403 again, but targeted chat reads worked for the Mar, Jorge, and EPMO Liderazgo threads.

items.label: Turn the KPI set into SMART goals
items.detail: This remains the one urgent carry-over with a priority reaction in state. Use the protected midday block or 4:00 PM prep overlap to turn the KPI set into a small leadership-ready group of goals, not a loose metric list.

items.label: Update Jorge's project sheet for Jill's portfolio
items.detail: Jorge confirmed the Jill portfolio meeting is Tuesday, June 30. Finish the tracker update with the active, rejected, completed, and IT-evaluation split, and resolve the hidden-row question before the data gets stale.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day has a compact morning 1:1 run, a usable 11:30 AM-1:30 PM protected block, then a dense 1:30-5:00 PM sequence with overlap at 4:00 PM between meeting prep and CoE.

items.label: 1:1 David & Jean
items.detail: Good early checkpoint for CoE, TRACE, or any leadership follow-through that needs David's input before the afternoon stack.

items.label: 1:1 Carolina and Jean
items.detail: Department support and project-review conversation; use it to catch any new intake or support asks.

items.label: focus time
items.detail: Short protected block before the longer blocked space. Best use is KPI SMART-goal cleanup or reviewing the Jorge tracker questions.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best focus window of the day. Protect it for the KPI goals, Jorge's project sheet, or prep for the 1:30 PM transition conversation.

items.label: Jorge|Jean TM's transition plan
items.detail: Jorge's invite asks for current schedules, approved time off, performance feedback, and skills or experience context before team-member transition.

items.label: 1:1 Yuri & Jean
items.detail: Use this to confirm TRACE taskset follow-through, any DevOps or BA dependency, and where the TRACE follow-on work should sit.

items.label: 1:1 Andrea and Jean
items.detail: PM checkpoint immediately after Yuri; useful for Phase 2 handoffs or work that should not wait for tomorrow's team forum.

items.label: LeadUp Module 2: Team Development
items.detail: Leadership block focused on moving from diagnosis to development. It leads directly into the 4:00 PM overlap.

items.label: Focus time: weekly meeting preparation / CoE Biweekly Touchpoint
items.detail: Outlook shows weekly prep overlapping the CoE touchpoint with David, Frank, and Mar. Decide whether the prep block can yield to CoE today.

items.label: Quick Touchpoint
items.detail: Quick sync with Mar and Jhara after CoE. Good place to close MHE/RFE support and CoE capacity questions before the day ends.

items.label: Decide whether Mar gets PE support for MHE/RFE
items.detail: Mar told Jean she is handling MHE/RFE, already gave Angel a heads-up with a July 3 update target, and asked whether a PE can be assigned before people move to Eugenia. Use the quick touchpoint to decide support before this becomes a capacity issue.

items.label: Confirm the TRACE follow-on placement
items.detail: EPMO Liderazgo aligned that Creation of Fix Task Sets for Appeal and Refile Process should not sit in pending assignment; it should stay deferred until current TRACE is closed or in hypercare, then move naturally to pending prioritization if still needed.

items.label: Prepare Jorge's transition inputs before 1:30 PM
items.detail: Bring schedules, approved time off, performance feedback, and skills or experience context to Jorge's transition-plan call so the team-member handoff has clean facts instead of follow-up churn.

items.label: Use CoE touchpoints to settle capacity questions
items.detail: The CoE Biweekly Touchpoint and the Mar/Jhara quick touchpoint are the day's best forums for CoE direction, Mar's MHE load, and whether anything needs David, Frank, or Jhara before tomorrow's larger team meeting.

items.label: Track Angel's response on MHE/RFE
items.detail: Mar said she sent Angel a short heads-up that she is taking the RFE MHE project and would provide an update on July 3. Jean asked her to share Angel's response, since Jean was not copied.

items.label: Plan follow-ups for the July 1 team alignment
items.detail: Janet told EPMO Liderazgo that Thiago, Gustavo, and Nico agreed the new alignment makes sense and that it will be effective July 1. Jean said she would schedule time with them at the end of this week or early next week.

items.label: Camilo Vejarano is out through June 26
items.detail: Camilo's calendar response says he is on vacation from June 16 to June 26. Avoid assigning near-term follow-up that depends on him this week.

items.label: 1:1 Santiago & Jean
items.detail: Regular 1:1 before the midday protected block.

items.label: Jean Midyear
items.detail: Midyear meeting with Sara Watson; Jean has not responded in Outlook.

items.label: Weekly Meeting: BCP/Incident Response
items.detail: BCP/Incident Response touchpoint follows the midyear meeting and includes several legal and project stakeholders.

items.label: Eugenia & Jean PM/PE Transition Plan
items.detail: Transition conversation focused on Laura's projects, skills, and PM/PE movement.

items.label: Tudo Bem!'s Weekly Meeting
items.detail: Main team forum for project progress, blockers, and strategic initiative momentum.

items.label: IT Litify Sprint #13 Planning
items.detail: Sprint planning with IT Software, project managers, and stakeholders to review upcoming user stories.
